# PRD Alignment Checklist: Agent Resolve Documentation Entry

**Purpose**: Validate whether the requirements fully capture the PRD-level product design for structured agent-facing documentation resolution.
**Created**: Saturday, May 16, 2026
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Product Positioning

- [x] CHK001 Is the P0 agent-facing role of structured resolution stated as the primary product path rather than one feature among several? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK002 Is the CLI-first boundary explicitly preserved so the requirements cannot be interpreted as introducing MCP as the main product path? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK003 Are the responsibilities of resolve, fetch, and search separated clearly enough to prevent future requirements from assigning structured correctness to fuzzy search? [Clarity, Spec §User Stories 1-3, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK004 Is the target caller described as an AI agent that already has or can extract structured API intent from context? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Assumptions]

## Resolver Scope

- [x] CHK005 Are the valid structured intent shapes fully enumerated and bounded? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK006 Are unsupported or invalid structured intent shapes required to fail as structured errors rather than falling back to natural-language search? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK007 Are the resolver input fields complete against the PRD, including framework, symbol, type, member, member kind, source family, JSON preference, and caller identity? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK008 Is the v1 source-family scope constrained to Apple API documentation while preserving future expansion for non-API families? [Scope, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK009 Are Help and App Store Connect boundaries explicitly excluded from resolver v1 while still covered by search and fetch? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Evidence And Confidence

- [x] CHK010 Is fetch verification defined as a necessary precondition for high confidence across direct and fallback candidates? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-009, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK011 Are the confidence states and their meanings defined enough for an agent caller to distinguish high, medium, low, and unresolved outcomes? [Spec §FR-025, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK012 Are resolver diagnostics and fetch diagnostics specified as distinct requirement surfaces rather than a generic diagnostic bucket? [Clarity, Spec §FR-027, Spec §FR-028, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK013 Are ambiguity requirements defined for multiple fetch-verified candidates, including what the caller can inspect? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK014 Is the evidence payload specified enough to support downstream agent claims without forcing the plan to infer missing evidence semantics? [Spec §FR-026, Spec §Key Entities]

## Search Fallback Boundary

- [x] CHK015 Is search fallback described as candidate recovery only, with clear limits on when it may affect confidence? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK016 Are wrong-framework and wrong-type fallback cases covered as low-confidence or unresolved outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK017 Are natural-language, typo, and broad discovery search cases framed as exploration-quality requirements rather than resolver-correctness requirements? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-022]
- [x] CHK018 Is search compatibility preserved without implying that search output must gain resolver-only fields? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-004]

## Audit And Issue Handling

- [x] CHK019 Is the benchmark reframing captured as a capability-layered evidence quality audit with resolve, search, and fetch as primary dimensions? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-017, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK020 Are resolve correctness audit requirements tied to exact symbol and member API examples across the required Apple framework areas? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK021 Are fetch evidence audit requirements distinct from resolver candidate discovery requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK022 Is automatic P0 issue creation explicitly limited to resolve and fetch golden-truth failures? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-009]
- [x] CHK023 Are the PRD-specific outcomes for issue #11 and issue #12 explicitly represented, rather than only summarized as generic existing issue reclassification? [Spec §FR-029, Spec §FR-030, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK024 Is the old all-search fingerprint closure standard explicitly replaced by capability-layered audit closure criteria? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-023]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK025 Are resolver smoke-case success criteria measurable without depending on implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK026 Are invalid-intent criteria measurable enough to distinguish structured error handling from silent search fallback? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK027 Are compatibility criteria broad enough to cover existing search, fetch, and list consumers without weakening resolver requirements? [Coverage, Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK028 Is the audit report usability criterion specific enough to validate maintainer understanding of resolve, fetch, and search failure categories? [Measurability, Spec §SC-010]

## Dependencies And Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 Are assumptions about agent-side intent extraction explicit enough to keep NLP, embeddings, and Google-like search out of resolver v1 scope? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK030 Are shared service boundary and mock/contract-test expectations stated without leaking implementation design into the PRD? [Boundary, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK031 Are backward compatibility assumptions for existing outputs clear enough to prevent breaking existing consumers during planning? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK032 Is the current absence of plan.md/tasks.md acknowledged as a phase boundary so PRD alignment is not mistaken for implementation readiness? [Phase Boundary, Spec §plan.md, Spec §tasks.md]

## Notes

- Generated as a requirements-quality checklist, not an implementation verification checklist.
- Focus areas selected: PRD alignment, resolver scope, evidence confidence, search fallback boundary, audit and issue handling, compatibility.
- Depth level: Standard PR reviewer gate before `/speckit.plan`.
